primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité, en comptes consolidés, d’une relation de couverture sur le risque de change lié à des dividendes intragroupe / à la créance intragroupe correspondante, et identification du modèle de comptabilité de couverture pertinent.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge-accounting eligibility and model questions, so IFRS 9 governs this issue and IAS 39 is discarded for overlap.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low; the core issue is hedge-accounting eligibility and hedge type selection, which IFRS 9 directly governs, while IAS 21 remains relevant only as supporting authority for foreign-currency effects in consolidation.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which intragroup items may be hedged in consolidated financial statements and identify the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain when intragroup monetary items create exchange differences in consolidation and when such items may form part of a net investment.
This interpretation constrains and clarifies the hedge of a net investment model, including the hedged risk and where hedging instruments may be held within a group.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models available for foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements
Intragroup foreign currency exposures that may survive consolidation and therefore potentially enter hedge accounting analysis
Net investment-specific hedge accounting for qualifying foreign currency exposure within a group

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as one of the three top-level hedge accounting models for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct top-level model for variability in cash flows, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate named hedge accounting model, further clarified by IAS 21 and IFRIC 16.
